title: Sleepy-time pillow spray
subtitle: A lavender, Roman chamomile, and cedarwood spray for pillows and bedlinen
excerpt: Lavender and Roman chamomile are the most studied essential oils for sleep support; cedarwood adds a grounding base note that slows the blend down. A light mist on pillows and sheets shortly before bed. The vodka disperses the oils and evaporates quickly.
sourceNotes: Lavender and sleep quality: Lillehei and Halcon, Journal of Alternative and Complementary Medicine (2014). Roman chamomile mild sedative properties: Srivastava et al., Molecular Medicine Reports (2010).

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Keep each bottle to 250 ml for correct concentration.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: This spray benefits from a week of resting after blending, which lets the oils meld.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Use within three months. Discard if the spray smells flat or medicinal -- lavender and chamomile oxidise and the pleasant floral note becomes harsh.

glossaryTerms.definition: Steam-distilled oil from Anthemis nobilis flowers. Delicate, sweet, apple-like scent. Very mild at 0.5 to 1% and one of the few essential oils considered gentle enough for sensitive skin.

content.text: , which adds a soft, sweet apple note that complements the lavender without competing with it. Cedarwood grounds the blend and gives it longevity on fabric. The vodka helps the oils disperse in water and evaporates in seconds, leaving only the fragrance.

Add all the essential oils to the 40 ml of vodka and swirl to combine. Add the distilled water. Pour into the amber spray bottle, cap, and shake well. The spray will appear slightly cloudy; shake before each use as the oils and water separate on standing.

Shake well. Hold the bottle 30 cm from the pillow and bedlinen and spray two to four times, directing the mist across the fabric. Apply 15 to 30 minutes before getting into bed so the alcohol evaporates before contact with skin. Keep away from eyes. Do not spray around cats.

Test on a hidden area of delicate fabrics before general use. The very low essential oil concentration in this spray is safe for cotton and linen bedding; the vodka evaporates quickly and does not leave a residue. Do not soak the fabric -- a fine mist is enough.

Keep at room temperature in the amber bottle, away from direct sunlight. Use within three months. When the lavender scent has become harsh, flat, or unpleasant, the oils have oxidised and the bottle should be discarded.
